label: Beijing
sub: Capital of PRC since 1949; Yuan, Ming & Qing imperial capital (Khanbaliq/Dadu/Beijing) from 1271 CE

label: Xi'an (Chang'an)
sub: Imperial capital of Qin, Western Han, and Tang Dynasty (618–907 CE); eastern terminus of Silk Road

label: Nanjing
sub: Capital of Ming Dynasty (1368–1421), Taiping Heavenly Kingdom (1853–1864), and Republic of China (1927–1949). Nanjing Massacre (1937): 200,000+ killed.

label: Luoyang
sub: Eastern Han capital (25–220 CE); served as capital under 13 dynasties. One of China's seven ancient capitals.

label: Kaifeng
sub: Northern Song Dynasty capital (960–1127 CE). Population of ~1 million; one of medieval world's largest cities. Captured by Jin Dynasty 1127.

label: Great Wall – Juyong Pass
sub: Most iconic Great Wall section (Ming Dynasty, 1368–1644 CE). Built to defend against Mongol and Manchu invasions. Total length: 8,850+ km.

label: Tiananmen Square
sub: Site of PRC founding proclamation (Oct 1, 1949) and 1989 pro-democracy massacre. World's largest public square. All commemoration of June 4 banned in mainland China.

label: Shanghai
sub: Treaty port opened after First Opium War (1842). CCP founded nearby (1921). Now China's financial capital and world's busiest container port.

label: Shenzhen SEZ
sub: China's first Special Economic Zone (1980), established by Deng Xiaoping. Population grew from 30,000 (1979) to 18 million (2024). Symbol of reform-era economic miracle.

label: Hong Kong
sub: Ceded to Britain (1842 Opium War). Handover to PRC (1997). 2019 pro-democracy protests (2M+ marched). National Security Law imposed June 2020.

label: Wuhan
sub: Epicenter of COVID-19 pandemic (Dec 2019). First lockdown city in history (Jan 23, 2020). Wuhan Institute of Virology nearby. ~3,869 official deaths during initial outbreak.

label: Kashgar – Xinjiang
sub: Historical Silk Road hub and Uyghur cultural center. Center of Chinese mass detention campaign (2017–present). Estimated 1M+ Uyghurs detained in 'vocational education centers' across the region.

label: Lhasa – Tibet
sub: Seat of Dalai Lama's government until Chinese military takeover (1950–51). 1959 Tibetan uprising drove Dalai Lama into exile in India. Potala Palace: UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Yan'an – Long March End
sub: Final destination of the Long March (1935). CCP base 1935–1947; birthplace of Maoist ideology and governance model. Now a major patriotic education pilgrimage site.

label: Ruijin – Jiangxi Soviet
sub: Starting point of the Long March (October 1934). Capital of Chinese Soviet Republic (1931–1934) before Nationalist encirclement forced CCP evacuation.

label: Nanchang Uprising
sub: Site of August 1, 1927 CCP uprising against Chiang Kai-shek's Nationalists—birthday of People's Liberation Army. Led by Zhu De and Zhou Enlai.

label: Dunhuang – Silk Road Oasis
sub: Key Silk Road junction and Buddhist pilgrimage site. Mogao Caves contain world's largest collection of Buddhist art (5th–14th century). Gateway between China and Central Asia.

label: Guangzhou (Canton)
sub: China's historical center of foreign trade. Guangzhou System restricted foreign trade before Opium Wars. Sun Yat-sen's base for Northern Expedition (1926). Modern manufacturing hub.

label: Macau
sub: Portuguese colony from 1557; handover to PRC on December 20, 1999. World's largest gambling revenue. Under 'One Country, Two Systems' like Hong Kong.

label: Taiwan Strait
sub: 160km-wide strait between PRC and Taiwan. PRC claims Taiwan as core territory. US 7th Fleet patrols under Taiwan Relations Act. Center of ongoing military tension (Joint Sword exercises 2024).

label: Yalu River – Korean War
sub: China-North Korea border river. PLA crossed here October 1950 to enter Korean War, pushing UN forces south. Battle of the Yalu River halted US-led advance toward Chinese territory.

label: Three Gorges Dam
sub: World's largest hydropower project (completed 2006). 22,500 MW capacity. Displaced 1.3 million people. Site of decades of debate over environmental impact and flood risk.

label: Chengdu – Sichuan
sub: Near epicenter of 2008 Sichuan earthquake (M7.9, 69,197 killed). Provincial capital of Sichuan. Historical capital of Shu Han (Three Kingdoms). Major tech hub in western China.

label: Harbin – Manchuria
sub: Japanese puppet state of Manchukuo capital (1932–1945). Site of Unit 731 biological warfare experiments. Chinese Civil War decisive battle of Liaoshen Campaign (1948) fought nearby.

label: Urumqi – Xinjiang Capital
sub: Capital of Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region. Site of 2009 ethnic riots (197 killed). Hub of China's mass surveillance and detention system targeting Uyghurs and other Turkic minorities.

label: Anyang – Shang Capital
sub: Ruins of Yin, last capital of Shang Dynasty (c. 1300–1046 BCE). Oracle bone inscriptions found here are China's oldest confirmed writing. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Paracel Islands – South China Sea
sub: Seized by China from South Vietnam in 1974 Battle of Paracel Islands. China claims entire South China Sea via 'nine-dash line.' Disputed by Vietnam, Philippines, Malaysia, Brunei, and Taiwan.

label: Chongqing
sub: Chinese wartime capital 1937–1946 during Japanese occupation of eastern China. Site of Bo Xilai political scandal (2012). Largest municipality in China by area (82,400 km²).

label: Dalian – Strategic Port
sub: Russian-leased (1898–1905), Japanese-controlled (1905–1945), Soviet-controlled (1945–1955). Major PLA Navy base and commercial port today. Key node in BRI maritime route.

label: Erlitou – Possible Xia Capital
sub: Archaeological site near Yanshi, Henan. Erlitou culture (c. 1900–1500 BCE) is the leading candidate for the legendary Xia Dynasty. Bronze foundries and palace foundations discovered.
